opp.ais_why:
• Across only 109 chars of NextStep ‚Äî a single entry ‚Äî the full content reads: 'See notes below RFP Expected in June, we're following up with them for a demo slot this month.' Zero AI dialogue captured.
• Product scope is the single SKU 'Agentic AI' ‚Äî a focused agentic-AI-only play, like the SCG Packaging opp. RFP is expected in June. BD_Engagement is Engaged. The opp is named 'EPF Malaysia' (Employees Provident Fund ‚Äî Malaysia's national retirement fund ‚Äî a major institutional buyer).
• Confidence is Low because the NextStep is essentially a single sentence. The Agentic AI-only product scope is a genuine signal that Zycus has positioned this as an AI deal, but with no buyer dialogue captured beyond a planned demo slot and a June RFP expectation, score 6 reflects an AI-positioned product scope without buyer voice yet ‚Äî would re-evaluate after the demo and RFP release.

The deal scope spans seven modules (iContract, iSource, iSupplier, eProcurement, eInvoicing, iSaaS, Agentic AI) which implies enterprise-wide procurement transformation ambition, but without a recorded discovery or workshop transcript the specific operational pain (cycle time, cost leakage, compliance risk, headcount) and the financial impact the buyer is solving for remain unknown. The next move is to secure a quantified business case from Lokman or the procurement lead and document it: target savings, headcount reduction, or cycle-time improvement tied to the seven-module scope.

sources: SF OpportunityContactRole: M. M. Lokman (Head of eProcurement System Unit), Decision Maker
SF Next_Step__c: 'working with Lokman to craft the RFP'
Avoma: 11 Jun 2026 AI demos (not recorded, Lokman engagement inferred from Next_Step)
SF OpportunityContactRole: Emil Fazly (Contract Management Specialist), Influencer
Task contacts (90d): Lokman, Mohamad Kassim, Ahmad Jefry Abd. Hashim, Chong Leng, Mohd Adzfar bin Mhd Nazli
champion.narrative: M. M. Lokman (Head of eProcurement System Unit, marked Decision Maker in Contact Roles) is the closest to a champion and is actively engaged: he is co-drafting the RFP with Zycus (per Next_Step__c 'working with Lokman to craft the RFP'), attended or was referenced in the 11 Jun AI-focused demos, and is the named point of contact for RFP follow-up. Co-authoring the RFP is strong evidence of advocacy—he is shaping the evaluation criteria in our favor. However, his role as 'Head of eProcurement System Unit' suggests he is a technical/operational Decision Maker, not an Economic Buyer or executive sponsor, so his ability to drive budget approval and executive alignment is uncertain. There is no evidence yet that he has introduced Zycus to senior leadership, provided internal political guidance, or actively blocked competitors. Emil Fazly (Contract Management Specialist, Influencer) is a second engaged contact but is not shown advocating. No other multi-threading is visible (only 3 Contact Roles total, and recent tasks reference only 5 buyer names across 72 days). The champion strength is developing: Lokman is engaged and co-creating the process, but he has not yet demonstrated full economic/executive access or competitive blocking behavior. The deal is partner-led so the partner may be the true champion intermediary. Next move: validate with the partner whether Lokman has executive sponsor access and can drive budget sign-off, and secure a direct or partner-brokered introduction to the Economic Buyer through Lokman.

competition.narrative: No competitor is named in any source. The Salesforce Competitors__c field is unknown/unread, the Next_Step__c makes no mention of another vendor, the four Avoma touchpoints were not recorded so no competitive benchmarking discussion is captured, and the Next_Step_History__c and completed Tasks (if any) contain no rival references. The buyer is running a formal RFP process ('RFP expected in June', 'working with Lokman to craft the RFP') which strongly implies a multi-vendor evaluation, and the co-drafting relationship with Zycus suggests Zycus is an early or preferred vendor, but whether this is a sole-source procurement or a competitive RFP with 2-3 shortlisted vendors is unknown. For a $200k seven-module deal at a government statutory body, a competitive process is highly likely. The unknowns: who else is in the RFP (SAP Ariba, Coupa, Ivalua, GEP, Jaggaer, or regional players like Oracle or local integrators), what their positioning is, and whether Zycus is the incumbent or a challenger. This is a critical blind spot. Next move: ask Lokman or the partner directly who else is being evaluated in the RFP and what their differentiation story is, and document it immediately so the competitive response can be tuned before RFP submission.

The Salesforce pain fields (Customer_Business_Problem__c, Compelling_Event__c, What_if_this_is_not_done__c, Pain_points_in_the_current_solution__c unread) are empty, and the four Avoma touchpoints (15 Apr intro, 11 Jun AI demos) were not recorded so no buyer-stated pain is available. The Next_Step__c references a 'Workshop showcasing our Agentic AI' on 9 Jun, suggesting the buyer is interested in AI-driven procurement intelligence or negotiation automation, but without a transcript the specific pain that capability solves (manual contract review, supplier price opacity, slow approval cycles) is unknown. Next move: obtain from Lokman or the partner the top operational pain driving this investment and document it with a quantified impact (e.g., contract review backlog, procurement cycle time, supplier consolidation target).

However, no MSA, SOW, or Order Form activity is documented, no InfoSec or ERP integration discussion is recorded (the Avoma demos were not transcribed), and the Salesforce paper-process fields (Mandate__c, Current_Contract_Expiration__c unread) offer no forcing function or incumbent contract timeline. No SI or partner implementation role is stated despite the partner-led sales motion (the APAC geography note suggests a channel partner is running the engagement). The gap: what is the contracting vehicle (MSA + Order Form, direct government procurement contract), what are the InfoSec and compliance requirements for a Malaysian government statutory body, is there an SI scoping the implementation, and is there an incumbent contract expiration date driving urgency. Next move: confirm with the partner and Lokman the contracting process, InfoSec review requirements, and any incumbent contract end-date, and surface those in the mutual close plan.

decision_process.narrative: The decision process is RFP-driven and involves a procurement and contracts team, but the full approval chain and timeline are not yet mapped. The Next_Step__c states '11th July - Met with their procurement and contracts team and showcased demo' (now ~2 weeks ago), confirming that procurement and legal/contracts are involved early, which is typical for a government buyer. The Avoma call sequence shows two introductory meetings on 15 Apr 2026 (deal creation date) followed by two AI-focused demos on 11 Jun 2026, a ~8-week gap that is normal for a large government RFP cycle. The buyer is co-drafting the RFP with Zycus (per Next_Step), which suggests Zycus has influence over the process design—a strong signal. However, key unknowns remain: who beyond Lokman and the procurement/contracts team will score the RFP, what the approval levels are (Does_the_Buyer_need_approval__c, Purchase_approvals_Required_from__c unread), whether an Economic Buyer or CFO must sign off, and what the post-RFP steps are (shortlist, POC, reference calls, contracting). The Contact Roles list only Lokman (DM) and Emil Fazly (Contract Management Specialist, Influencer), so the full committee is not yet visible. The deal is partner-led so the partner may hold additional process knowledge. Next move: map the full RFP evaluation committee, approval chain, and post-RFP timeline with Lokman and the partner.

decision_criteria.narrative: Some evaluation structure is visible but criteria detail is thin. The Next_Step__c states the team is 'working with Lokman to craft the RFP' (as of 11 Jul, now ~2 weeks ago) and 'RFP expected in June' (now past), which confirms the buyer is running a formal RFP-driven process and Zycus is co-drafting it with the Decision Maker—a strong positioning signal. The two 11 Jun 2026 demos were titled 'AI focused' and the Next_Step references a '2 hour Workshop showcasing our Agentic AI and other modules on 9th June', indicating AI/Agentic capability is a stated evaluation criterion alongside the six other modules in scope (iContract, iSource, iSupplier, eProcurement, eInvoicing, iSaaS). However, the demos were not recorded so the specific use-case requirements, weighting, and scoring rubric the buyer will apply in the RFP are unknown. The Salesforce criteria fields (Business_Requirements__c, Top_Challenges_Priorities__c, AI_Needs_in_RFP_Rating__c) are unread. The partial read: RFP-based evaluation, AI/Agentic capability confirmed as a criterion, and co-drafting the RFP with the DM is a wedge. The gap: no visibility into the full criteria set, relative weighting, or competitive scoring thresholds. Next move: obtain the draft RFP or BRD from Lokman to map every criterion and prepare tailored responses.

However, the buyer behavior signals AI Hungry: they explicitly requested an 'AI focused' demo (11 Jun 2026 Avoma titles) and a '2 hour Workshop showcasing our Agentic AI' (9 Jun 2026 per Next_Step__c), and Agentic AI is one of seven modules in the Products__c scope, indicating it is a formal RFP evaluation criterion, not a nice-to-have. Tier: AI Hungry.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 15 Apr 2026, 72 days in Qualified stage as of 26 Jun 2026. Last activity 13 Jun 2026 (13 days ago). Close date 30 Jan 2027 is 218 days out. Time-in-stage velocity is slow for an engaged deal: qualified-to-close typically 12-15 months in large enterprise motion, this deal has ~7 months runway remaining but has not yet formally entered RFP/evaluation despite buyer stating RFP expected in June 2026 (now past). No forward slip recorded in field history. Remaining path: RFP submission and response, shortlisting, deeper use-case validation, commercials, EB sign-off, contracting — realistic to complete in 7 months if RFP lands in July 2026, but stage must advance within 30 days to stay on pace.

north_star_verdict.headline: Partner-led EPF deal co-drafting RFP with Decision Maker Lokman, but June RFP deadline passed with no delivery and 13 days silent. Qualified stage at 72 days; must enter Formal Evaluation by mid-July to protect January close timeline.

best_practice_check.summary: Partner-led APAC deal at $200k with 218 days to close, live engagement through June 2026 but thin recorded coverage (0 Avoma transcripts). Co-drafting the RFP with the Decision Maker is a strong positioning wedge, but five critical gaps block forecast confidence: no EB mapped, no quantified ROI case, no competitive intelligence, thin multi-threading (3 contact roles), and no champion with confirmed executive/budget access. The immediate levers to raise win probability: (1) map and engage the EB through the partner or Lokman before commercials begin, (2) obtain the draft RFP and competitive shortlist from Lokman to tune the response and competitive wedge, (3) secure a quantified business case tied to the seven-module scope to anchor the pricing conversation and justify the $200k investment.

The buyer is running a formal RFP process which implies multi-vendor evaluation, and Zycus is co-drafting the RFP with Decision Maker Lokman, a strong early-mover positioning signal. However, without knowing who else is shortlisted (likely SAP Ariba, Coupa, GEP, or regional players given the $200k enterprise scope), threat assessment is impossible. If sole-source, Zycus is in control; if competitive, the co-drafting wedge must be defended and the competitive field mapped immediately. Current threat level: unknown. Wedge: RFP co-authoring with the Decision Maker gives Zycus influence over evaluation criteria and scoring, especially around Agentic AI differentiation.

This is a strong AI appetite signal and Zycus's Merlin/Agentic positioning is landing. The AI positioning is strong but thin on confirmation of buyer-stated value. Score: AI Hungry (explicitly evaluating AI as a criterion).

For a Malaysian statutory body (KWSP/EPF), budget authority likely sits with a CFO, CPO, or senior operations executive. Without EB mapping and sign-off, the forecast is indefensible. deltas.subject: Obtain the draft RFP and competitive shortlist from Lokman to sharpen the response: The buyer is co-drafting the RFP with Zycus (strong wedge), but the full criteria set, weighting, and competitive shortlist are unknown. No competitor is named in any source, yet the RFP process implies multi-vendor evaluation (likely SAP Ariba, Coupa, GEP, or regional players). Without knowing who Zycus is up against and what the scoring rubric prioritizes, the response and demo strategy cannot be tuned. Act within 7 days: request the draft RFP from Lokman, confirm the vendor shortlist, and map each competitor's known strengths so the Agentic AI and seven-module differentiation can be positioned against their gaps.

deltas.to: Build a quantified business case with Lokman and the EB to anchor pricing and ROI: No quantified pain, savings target, or financial impact is documented despite
deltas.subject: Build a quantified business case with Lokman and the EB to anchor pricing and ROI: No quantified pain, savings target, or financial impact is documented despite a $200k seven-module scope (iContract, iSource, iSupplier, eProcurement, eInvoicing, iSaaS, Agentic AI). The buyer is a government fund (KWSP/EPF) so ROI and compliance justification will be scrutinized. Without a documented value case (e.g., X% cycle-time reduction, Y FTE savings, Z cost avoidance), the pricing conversation and EB sign-off are at risk. Work with Lokman and the partner to quantify the operational and financial impact of the seven-module transformation, and document it in a joint ROI model within 21 days, before commercials begin.
